title: DORA TLPT — Findings Severity, Remediation Owner, and Due Date Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=grc` `sourcetype="tlpt:finding"` (finding_id, engagement_id, severity, owner, due_date, status, related_provider_id)
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable/attack platform JSON
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="tlpt:finding" status!="closed" earliest=-400d
| eval due=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due<now() OR isnull(owner)
| stats count by severity, related_provider_id, owner
| sort severity, - count
value: TLPT produces material findings. Monitoring remediation SLAs by severity with linkage to ICT third-party providers evidences closed-loop treatment required for resilience testing programs.
implementation: (1) Map severity to internal SLAs; (2) auto-create problems in ITSM; (3) escalate critical past-due to management body; (4) join provider_id to RoI; (5) quarterly export for regulator file.
visualization: Table (overdue), Column chart by severity, Single value (open critical).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
